mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output, which drives corporate earnings expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 50% of months following month-over-month INDPRO growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating household income and consumption demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when the trigger fires during any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous elevation in both the federal funds rate and the consumer price index signals restrictive real monetary policy that squeezes corporate margins and reduces equity present values, generating negative S&P 500 returns; the joint effect is stronger than either variable alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of joint-elevation months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, month-over-month strength in housing starts signals persistent demand pressures that compel the Federal Reserve to continue raising interest rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as discount rates rise faster than corporate cash flows.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal Reserve tightening cycle ongoing since June 2004
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when HOUST rises while the Federal Reserve is actively tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises short-term funding costs immediately, causing a rapid repricing of equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS increases, over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales demonstrates a self-reinforcing demand expansion where rising employment fuels consumption; this joint dynamic lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month and RSAFS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and RSAFS rise in the same month, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of such joint-signal months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points within two months signals tight labor markets and accelerating wage-driven consumption, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as growth optimism reprices.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances during any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: An outsized month-over-month increase in the consumer price index above 0.3 index points signals accelerating inflation that erodes real corporate earnings and prompts expectations of tighter Federal Reserve policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month by more than 0.3 index points
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.3 points, over any 3-year rolling window.
